marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time: 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time: 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
bloom_levels: Domain 1 - Remembering and Understanding: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Domain 2 - Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Domain 3 - Analysing, Evaluating and Creating: Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.
internal_assessment: Class XI Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Students: Identification/Familiarity with the apparatus (5 Marks), Written test based on prescribed practicals (10 Marks), Practical Record (5 Marks), Viva (10 Marks). Total 30 Marks.
Written examination in practicals conducted at time of practical examination. Duration: 30 minutes. Question paper contains 15 skill-based very short answer questions; student answers any 10.
practical_assessment: A. Basic Laboratory Techniques (Cutting, Bending, Drawing glass tube/jet, Boring cork)
B. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances (Melting point, Boiling point, Crystallization)
C. Experiments based on pH (Determination of pH, Comparing strong/weak acids, Titration study, Common-ion effect)
D. Chemical Equilibrium (Shift in equilibrium studies)
E. Quantitative Estimation (Balance usage, Standard solution preparation, Titrations for strength determination)
F. Qualitative Analysis (Anion/Cation detection in salts, Detection of N/S/Cl in organics)
A. Surface Chemistry (Preparation of sols, Dialysis, Emulsifying agents)
B. Chemical Kinetics (Effect of concentration/temp on rate, Reaction rates studies)
C. Thermochemistry (Enthalpy of dissolution/neutralization/hydrogen bond formation)
D. Electrochemistry (Variation of cell potential with electrolyte concentration)
E. Chromatography (Separation of pigments/inorganic mixtures, Rf values)
F. Preparation of Inorganic Compounds (Double salts, Potassium Ferric Oxalate)
G. Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow/dye)
H. Tests for functional groups in organic compounds
I. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins
J. Determination of concentration/molarity (KMnO4 titration against Oxalic acid/Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate)
project_assessment: Suggested Projects: Bacterial contamination in drinking water, Purification of water methods, Hardness/Iron/Fluoride testing, Foaming capacity of soaps, Acidity of tea leaves, Rate of evaporation, Effect of acids/bases on fibers, Acidity of fruit juices.

Suggested Projects: Oxalate ions in guava, Casein quantity in milk, Soybean milk preparation, Potassium Bisulphate as preservative, Digestion of starch by salivary amylase, Fermentation rate comparison, Extraction of essential oils (Saunf/Ajwain/Illaichi), Food adulterants study.
Note: Any other investigatory project involving about 10 periods can be chosen with teacher approval.
